perturbation.original_text: "Lead-time" is defined as the amount of time between Manufacturer receiving an order and EMV receipt of the goods ordered. The ordering processes are listed in Section 4.
perturbation.changed_text: "Lead-time" is defined as the amount of time between Manufacturer commencing production after receiving an order and the time goods are available for shipment. The ordering processes are listed in Section 4.
perturbation.explanation: The changed definition of 'Lead-time' now focuses on production time rather than the entire process from order to receipt by EMV. This contradicts later sections, such as Section 4.4, where Lead-time is used as a factor in on-time delivery calculations, thus creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Response Time. Manufacturer shall make commercially reasonable efforts to manufacture and deliver Products in accordance with the Purchase Orders issued by EMV. If Manufacturer is unable to meet the del ivery schedule set forth in a Purchase Order, Manufacturer shall notify EMV within_seven (_7_) business days following EMV's issuance of such Purchase Order. If Manufacturer subsequently becomes aware of circumstances that may lead to delays in delivery, Manufacturer shall notify EMV as soon as reasonably possible. The Manufacturer will make commercially reasonable efforts to deliver Products on or prior to the delivery date indicated on the Purchase Order (the "Delivery Target"). In order for a Product to be included as an on time delivery each Product needs to also meet all Specifications. The assessment of whether the Delivery Target has been achieved shall be calculated on a per shipment basis.
perturbation.justification: The changed definition of 'Lead-time' shifts the focus from the time between receiving an order to EMV receipt to the time between commencing production and when goods are available for shipment. This contradicts the use of 'Lead-time' in Section 4.4, where it's a factor in on-time delivery calculations. The original definition included the time for delivery to EMV, which is essential for determining if the 'Delivery Target' was achieved, as stated in the 'Contradicted Text'. By excluding the delivery time to EMV from the definition of Lead-time, it makes it impossible to assess on-time delivery accurately, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The manufacturing license granted in this Agreement is exclusive within the Territory.
perturbation.changed_text: The manufacturing license granted in this Agreement is exclusive within the Territory for electric passenger vehicles only.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'for electric passenger vehicles only' limits the scope of exclusivity. This contradicts the general understanding of exclusivity established in other sections, potentially allowing the Manufacturer to produce other types of electric vehicles, thus creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Manufacturing License License to Specifications. Subject to the terms of this Agreement, subject to Manufacturer meeting EMV's requirements for quality, price and lead- time, EMV hereby grants Manufacturer an exclusive, non-transferable, license (without the right to sublicense) under EMV's Proprietary Rights in the Territory, during the term of this Agreement, to use the Specifications solely for the purpose of manufacturing the Products to fulfil Purchase Orders for EMV. * Confidential treatment has been requested for certain portions of this Exhibit. The confidential portions of this Exhibit have been omitted and filed separately with the Securities and Exchange Commission. Such portions have been marked with "****" at the exact place where material has been omitted. 2 License to EMV Firmware. Subject to the terms of this Agreement, subject to Manufacturer meeting EMV's requirements for quality, price and lead- time, EMV hereby grants Manufacturer an exclusive, non-transferable, license (without the right to sublicense) under EMV's Proprietary Rights in the Territory, during the term of this Agreement, to copy the EMV firmware as may be provided by EMV from time to time onto Product units in the manufacturing process at each EMV-approved Manufacturer manufacturing facility. Subject to the terms of this Agreement, EMV grants to Manufacturer and Manufacturer accepts, for the term of this Agreement, the right to manufacture the Products only in the Territory as necessary to fulfil Purchase Orders for Products made by EMV, provided that such manufacturing is at Manufacturer's own cost for the purchase of the components of each order as well as assembling cost for finished products and in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text grants an 'exclusive' manufacturing license within the Territory. The changed text limits this exclusivity to 'electric passenger vehicles only'. The contradicted text grants an 'exclusive' license to manufacture 'the Products'. By narrowing the exclusivity to only electric passenger vehicles, the change now allows for the potential manufacture of other products that would previously have been covered by the original exclusive license, thereby contradicting the intent and scope of the original grant as outlined in the contradicted section. This creates ambiguity and a direct conflict regarding the scope of the exclusive license.

perturbation.original_text: The Manufacturer shall promptly replace or repair, at its sole expense, any defective Products arising from the assembly or manufacturing by the Manufacturer due to failure of the set Standard and Specification within the Product Warranty Period, including without limitations related shipping expenses.
perturbation.changed_text: The Manufacturer shall, after a mutually agreed upon investigation, replace or repair any defective Products arising from the assembly or manufacturing by the Manufacturer due to failure of the set Standard and Specification within the Product Warranty Period, including without limitations related shipping expenses. The expense of such replacement or repair, including shipping, shall be shared equally by both parties, unless the defect is proven to be caused by EMV's faulty designs.
perturbation.explanation: The modified clause introduces a shared expense model for defective product replacement/repair. This contradicts the original text where the Manufacturer bears the sole expense. This will affect Section 2.2.4 where the manufacturer warrants to EMV that the Products assembled or manufactured by Manufacturer will (i) conform in all respects to their Specifications, thus creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Manufacturer warrants to EMV that the Products assembled or manufactured by Manufacturer will (i) conform in all respects to their Specifications; (ii) will be merchantable, of good material and workmanship, with respect to such assembly or manufacture under normal use and service for three (3) years from the manufacture and assembly of the Products, not including the easily worn parts, list to be confirmed by both parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text shifts the financial responsibility for defective product replacement/repair from the Manufacturer's sole expense to a shared expense model. This directly contradicts the Manufacturer's warranty in the 'Contradicted Text' which guarantees that products conform to specifications and are of good workmanship, implying the manufacturer is fully responsible for defects within the warranty period. The change introduces a condition where EMV could be financially liable for the Manufacturer's failures to uphold its warranty, thus creating an in-text contradiction.
